key_insight: The paper replaces hard, combinatorial topological constraints with differentiable Hodge-spectral surrogates that capture homology through low-frequency Laplacian modes.
engineering_relevance: This enables gradient-based optimization of shapes, point clouds, and graph complexes while preserving target topology in a smoother and more scalable way.
method: Build soft graph and soft clique-complex operators using Hodge-Laplacian-type relaxations.
Use low-pass spectral losses such as heat filters, resolvent filters, and polynomial Laplacian moments to encode Betti-like objectives.
In the hard limit, the relaxed operator recovers the usual Hodge Laplacian on the active subcomplex.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that Hodge spectral-filter losses produce more spatially distributed gradients, smoother scale-normalized behavior, and geometry-aware updates than persistent-homology-based losses.
watch_item: These spectral surrogates are approximations, so choosing penalties, filter scales, and polynomial order may strongly affect how faithfully the target topology is enforced.
paper_title: Hodge Spectral Surrogates for Topology-Constrained Optimization
